title: Rumors Fly Sony or Warner Bros got kharas new Eva maybe
display_title: Rumors Fly Sony or Warner Bros got kharas new Eva maybe
wikitext:
[]: Rumors Fly, Sony or Warner Bros. got khara's new Eva, maybe?             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 9:27 am Zac Bertschy wrote: So has anyone licensed the EVA remake yet and when will it be released!?!? Nobody has officially announced the license yet, but all the rumors I've heard point to big mainstream studios; most of the scuttlebutt going around involves either Sony or Warner Bros., but obviously neither of them have made any sort of real announcement yet. We'll just have to wait and see. Whoever licenses it, I pray for a nice Bluray release. For a while there everyone assumed the license would wind up with ADV Films, if only because they held the license to the TV series for so long and the show kind of "made" them, but also because the film was distributed by KlockWorx, which is part of the ARM corporation, which ADV was affiliated with until recently (KlockWorx was responsible for production on series like Moonlight Mile and a bunch of other titles formerly licensed by ADV). Obviously that deal fell through and frankly the licensing cost for Rebuild of Evangelion was likely astronomical and put it outside the reach of most R1 anime distributors. If it does wind up at a place like Sony or Warner, hopefully they'll retain the original dub cast (I say that on behalf of the EVA dub's many fans; I myself have never liked the dub for that show, and found it destroyed a lot of the emotional complexity of the show with unpolished, unsubtle acting) and will load it up with special features. Speaking of, I found it very strange that AX was showing this film multiple times this year and yet the big main event was the premiere of Batman: Gotham Knight, which to me seems like less of a big deal to anime fans than a new Evangelion movie. I realize the film had been shown in the US once or twice before, but it's not like it was widely accessible to fans in any way, unless you count fansubs, in which case Batman doesn't have much of an edge considering DVD rips had leaked to the internet at least a week before the premiere at AX.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 10:00 am it wouldn't surprise me that GAINAX would go to non-traditional licensees solely on the "money" issue the interesting thing in all this is how the big boys like Sony or WB would handle this have either of them even handled a Japan-made anime before, or any other western media conglomo besides Disney (News Corp., Viacom, Vivendi, GE, etc.)? *NOTICE*: you can re-edit the title in your post too, cat42

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Jul 11, 2008 10:12 am hmmm it does make sense that Rebuild will be such a guaranteed mega-hit that Gainax has set the licensing fee astronomically high, resulting in not just ADV, but NO NORTH AMERICAN DUB COMPANY WHATSOEVER being able to afford it, and leaving it to the Big Dogs.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 10:14 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: it wouldn't surprise me that khara would go to non-traditional licensees solely on the "money" issue

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 10:23 am cat42 wrote: I wrote: <snip> have either of them even handled a Japan-made anime before, or any other western media conglomo besides Disney (News Corp., Viacom, Vivendi, GE, etc.)?

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Fri Jul 11, 2008 10:28 am cat42 wrote: Sony licensed Paprika & Blood+.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 10:46 am Anyway, I'm hoping this is true, because we're more likely to get a crap load of extras if it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 11, 2008 11:12 am I hope this is true myself, if only because I hope it means the subs/etc will probably be of better quality.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 11:13 am I have a feeling if it is true, the subs will be the Film Festival ones.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 11, 2008 11:15 am That'd be great. I can't help but wonder if those are the subs for the Region-3 release.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 11:32 am same here, honestly.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Jul 11, 2008 1:46 pm I read a story, can't remember where, that said something about Anno wanting to do an uncommon release of Weva in the west, probably something... free?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jul 11, 2008 1:52 pm AchtungAffen wrote: I read a story, can't remember where, that said something about Anno wanting to do an uncommon release of Weva in the west, probably something... free?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 3:29 pm AchtungAffen wrote: I read a story, can't remember where, that said something about Anno wanting to do an uncommon release of Weva in the west, probably something... free?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 11, 2008 4:47 pm So do you think we can hope for a limited theatrical release of EVA if it's Sony or Dubba-Dubba-WB? Of course, I'm not really that keen on having an environment like with the FMA or Death Note films, with a lot of fans "WOOOOO"ing whenever a character shows up or says anything even remotely suggestive... I'd be fully expecting retarded "IT'S TEH Rei" comments and overwhelming cheering when the first bookend Rei shows up. Anyone who saw it at a convention, was it like that there?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 5:06 pm NAveryW wrote: So do you think we can hope for a limited theatrical release of EVA if it's Sony or Dubba-Dubba-WB? <snip>

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 9:56 pm Yeah, but those were one to two night screenings, or something like that. If it is licensed by Sony or WB, then I hope it gets a couple week run, like Ghost in the Shell 2: Innocence, or Paprika did. (Plus both were shown in the Boston area with subtitles!)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 11, 2008 9:59 pm AA: That's mentioned in a recent Eva Mailing List post, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sun Jul 20, 2008 2:46 pm This rumor makes me ask the question, will this be the foot in the door for a live action Eva release?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Jul 21, 2008 1:31 am One can only hope.....NOT
